chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – Al-Tala’a (one-third residue of evaporated grape juice)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/283)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/283)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ الْعَصِيرَ إِذَا طُبِخَ حَتَّى يَذْهَبَ ثُلُثَاهُ وَ يَبْقَى ثُلُثُهُ فَهُوَ حَلَالٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, form his father, form Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The (grape) juice, when it is cooked until two-thirds of it evaporates and there remains a third of it, so it is Permissible’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘ Abd Allah ibn al-Mughirah from ‘ Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If juice (grape juice) is boiled until its two-thirds are evaporated and only one-third has remained, it then is lawful for use.’”
